hooks.prompt:
Permission policy is active. When ANY tool call is prompted or denied, classify it using this framework (in order):

1. Can it destroy unrecoverable data? (rm -rf, git push --force, git reset --hard, DROP TABLE) → DENY
2. Can it bypass permission rules? (node -e, python -c, bash -c, eval) → DENY
3. Does it affect shared state visible to others? (git push, gh pr create, curl POST) → ASK FIRST
4. Does it change local context in hard-to-undo ways? (git checkout <branch>, git merge, mv, kill) → ASK FIRST
5. Is it read-only, local-only, or easily reversible via git? (reads, searches, build, test, lint, git add/commit) → ALLOW

When uncertain, default to ASK FIRST.

Behavior: Note each prompted/denied command in ONE line (e.g. 'Noted: `pnpm audit` → allow, read-only'). Do NOT update settings mid-session. Continue with the task.

Pattern format: Bash(<command prefix>:*) — always use specific prefixes. NEVER add broad wildcards like Bash(npx:*), Bash(node:*), or Bash(bash:*).

At session end or when asked, invoke /manage-permissions to cross-reference all tools used against allow lists and propose a batch update.

Before ending: cross-reference every tool you used this session against the allow lists in ~/.claude/settings.json and .claude/settings.local.json. If ANY tool you used is missing from both allow lists, invoke /manage-permissions to propose a batch update. If every tool is already covered, say nothing.
